message:
So I'm that person who has a laminated escape plan taped inside every bedroom closet, and my family absolutely roasts me for it. But last month my neighbor's stove caught fire—nothing huge, caught fast—and when I was helping her think through what to do next, I realized I could actually walk her through *our* escape route because I'd thought about it that many times.

I'm not saying you need laminated diagrams (though hey, no judgment). But I *am* saying that thinking through "what if the kitchen fills with smoke" or "where would we actually go" is one of those prep things that costs nothing and lives rent-free in your head forever after. Plus, working smoke alarms are genuinely non-negotiable—I test ours every month because I've somehow made it my love language.

This little refresher on home fire safety actually made a lot of this feel less like doomsday planning and more like... just good sense. Which, honestly, changed how I talk about it with people.

Do you have an escape plan in place, even just a rough mental one?
